behaviors.description: Command segmentation treats newline-separated statements as separate segments, not one monolithic block.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local parsing/segmentation logic on one input string. Behavior text is about statement splitting itself, with no required module handoff, filesystem, subprocess, network, or model output. Rejected Integration because no concrete cross-module dependency is required by this behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Call the segmentation function with a multiline shell block (assignments on separate lines plus a command) and assert it returns discrete segments per newline statement rather than one combined segment.

behaviors.description: A multi-line shell block with variable assignments followed by `gh pr create` is recognized as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: Real failure appeared when segmentation output fed trigger classification and the whole block became `not_a_trigger`; this is a module-boundary handoff bug (segmenter -> trigger detector). No real OS/network/subprocess is needed to reproduce; no AI output dependency. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text only concerns local hook parsing/classification of command text.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real segmenter + trigger classifier together. Feed a multiline block with newline-separated assignments and trailing `gh pr create ...`; assert final classification is PR-create trigger (not `not_a_trigger`).

behaviors.description: A similar multi-line block that ends with `gh issue create` is not incorrectly treated as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: This is the negative path of the same boundary condition: segmentation + command-type classification across modules. Needs real wiring to ensure multiline parsing does not over-match trigger rules. Rejected higher levels because no real infrastructure or model decision is part of this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run integrated segmenter/classifier on multiline assignments plus trailing `gh issue create ...`; assert classification is non-PR trigger (e.g., `not_a_trigger` or issue-trigger), specifically not PR-create.

behaviors.description: When a PR-create trigger is detected, the hook can reconstruct and persist the PR identifier needed by the review loop state.
behaviors.justification: Behavior spans trigger result -> identifier reconstruction -> persisted state contract, involving multiple local modules and filesystem persistence. Local temp filesystem is sufficient; no real network/subprocess required by behavior text. Rejected System because no requirement here to execute `gh` or rely on OS-level timing/events.
behaviors.test_description: In an integration test with temp state directory, provide a detected PR-create event payload/command context, run hook path that reconstructs PR identifier, then assert state file exists and stores the expected PR identifier fields.

behaviors.description: After PR-create detection, the review gate state is written in a form that the pre-tool enforcement hook can read and enforce.
behaviors.justification: This is a cross-module contract test between PostToolUse writer and PreToolUse reader/enforcer using shared local state. Failure appears at module interaction boundary (serialization/schema/field semantics). Rejected Workflow because there are no multiple agentic model decisions; rejected System because in-process invocation plus temp filesystem can expose the format mismatch.
behaviors.test_description: Execute PostToolUse path to write gate state after PR-create trigger, then invoke PreToolUse enforcement logic against that same state store and assert enforcement activates as expected (e.g., blocks/allows according to gate rules).
